ops.title: Four live-session bugs fixed: revising dangle, round intent, watch cursors, tailnet dupes
ops.body: Session arc (2026-07-20/21), all shipped green: (1) revising working set now clears wholesale at submit/round.started in all three reducer ports + revising --clear (5b49b6c, shipped in v0.14.1); (2) tailnet display URLs collapse to one per name on a canonical port (d697622, v0.14.1) and advertise nothing when all legs are stale (86ddd3c, v0.14.2); (3) cc-interact v0.14.0: per-watcher watch-<pid> cursors seeded from max sibling with dead-pid GC, atomic cursor writes, persist-failure warn-not-fatal, watchers: N in status (cc-interact 205faad; bump ac9e08e); (4) explicit round intent: --round current|new required when a new top-level block lands in a dirty+engaged round, round.started carries unanswered blocks so nothing freezes, --round-title (68e61ea, v0.14.2). Adversarial review (finder+refuter per repo) confirmed 3 more fixes shipped in 86ddd3c: carry ids skip (never error — replay poison) when stale, cleared choice/empty input count unanswered for carry, child-promoted-to-top-level trips the guard. Deferred with criteria: cc-present f258295 (transactional two-append), cc-interact fb14bf0 (Activity.polls growth, split-lock status snapshot, watch-<pid> exact-match latency). E2E: all five behaviors PASS on a dev daemon (evidence in session transcript). Design reversal worth remembering: reducer-side validation of daemon-computed event payloads must tolerate, not error — the daemon's 8-worker dispatch pool means snapshots race appends, and a reduction error is permanent replay failure.
